title: comparisons, cross-type error, total compare
manager: Comparisons are total — every pair of same-type values orders definitively, with NaN handled explicitly — and comparing across incompatible types is a compile error, not a silent `false`. JavaScript's coercing equality is a decade of bugs in one operator; FSL refuses the coercions and makes "is this less than that" a question with a real, checkable answer instead of a surprise that depends on which operand came first.
cs: total order per type; cross-type comparison is a type error (no coercion); explicit NaN handling; underpins case guards and container sort.
